title: טוקיו
excerpt: מדריך טוקיו לישראלים שיסדר את החלטות הכניסה, הביטחון, הבחירה בין HND ל-NRT, התחבורה, אזור הלינה, העונה, המשפחה, הנגישות, התרופות, המכס והקישוריות על בסיס מקורות רשמיים ואימות עדכני לפני נסיעה.
author: מערכת Tra-Vel
primaryTopic: Tokyo travel guide for Israeli travelers
reviewMethod: Complete editorial-review article awaiting a named editor. Reopen every Japanese, Israeli, airport, operator and Tokyo authority at publication time and recheck every entry, warning, terminal, route, ticket, accessibility, weather, medicine, customs, accommodation, insurance and connectivity claim before any publication decision.

facts.claim: Japan's current visa-exemption list includes Israel for short temporary visits, but nationality, passport type, purpose and current bilateral conditions must be reopened before publication and departure.
The short-stay exemption is a temporary-visitor framework and does not authorize income-earning activity, so work, study and non-tourism purposes require separate confirmation.
A visa or visa exemption does not itself guarantee entry; the traveler remains subject to landing examination and must be able to explain the intended activities and stay.
Travelers need a valid passport and any visa required for their circumstances, and unusual travel documents or passport conditions require direct embassy and immigration confirmation.
Visit Japan Web is the official digital service for advance immigration and customs information and arrival codes, but travelers need a current official account flow and an offline document fallback.
The live National Security Council collector is the authority for the current Japan travel-warning level and must be checked at publication time, booking time and immediately before travel.
The Israeli Embassy in Tokyo publishes the official consular contact channel for Israeli travelers, and the current contact details and availability must be saved and rechecked before departure.
Haneda provides train, monorail, bus and taxi access choices whose suitability depends on the arrival terminal, destination address, luggage, arrival time and mobility needs.
Haneda has multiple terminal-transfer methods and eligibility distinctions, so a connection plan must use the actual arriving and departing terminals rather than a generic HND assumption.

Haneda offers requestable and reservable assistance in defined terminal and parking areas, while its published terms exclude boarding-pass-only gate areas; terminal assistance alone therefore does not prove coverage for the aircraft and every handoff requires separate confirmation.
Narita provides rail, bus, taxi, car and terminal-transfer choices, and the correct option depends on terminal, Tokyo area, transfer burden, luggage and service status.
Narita rail access uses terminal-specific stations and walking routes, so the traveler must match the airline terminal to the correct station and allow for the terminal route.
Narita accepts advance assistance arrangements and separates airport, railway and airline touchpoints, requiring a need-specific confirmation for every handoff.
HND and NRT must be compared by the full door-to-door chain, including terminal, Tokyo neighborhood, departure hour, transfer count and luggage, not by airport name or headline journey time alone.
Haneda and Narita are distinct airports with separate access systems, so an airport change or separate-ticket connection requires a ground-transfer plan and a conservative recheckable buffer.
JR East presents Narita Express as a reserved airport train serving several major Tokyo areas with luggage facilities, making final-area fit more important than a universal best claim.
Keisei presents Skyliner as a reserved airport train oriented to Nippori and Ueno with luggage facilities, so its value depends on the onward route to the selected area.
Tokyo Monorail connects Haneda terminals with the Hamamatsucho interchange, and the decision must include the onward operator, transfer path and final address.
Keikyu provides a separate Haneda rail architecture with terminal stations and onward connections, so it must be compared against the monorail using the actual final area.
Tokyo Metro distinguishes local tickets, IC-card use and visitor products with different eligibility and coverage, so the article must teach a route-led break-even method without freezing fares.
Welcome Suica can support everyday transit and payments but has area, product, validity and limited-express boundaries that must be rechecked before recommending it.
Tokyo Metro exposes station-specific barrier-free route, elevator, toilet and mobility filters, so accessible planning must use the exact station and exit rather than a network-wide promise.
The Japan Meteorological Agency provides multilingual weather and disaster-risk information for weather, heavy rain, heat, earthquakes, tsunami and volcanoes and must be treated as a live source.
Japan's Ministry of the Environment publishes current heat alerts and WBGT information, so summer pacing must be based on live conditions rather than seasonal averages alone.
Official tourism guidance distinguishes seasonal heat, humidity, rain, typhoon and cooler-weather patterns, but itinerary decisions require a Tokyo-specific forecast close to travel.

Tokyo's official emergency sources support a pre-downloaded response plan, following local instructions, avoiding an uninformed rush outside after an earthquake, and remaining in a safe place while checking authoritative disaster and transport information.

JNTO recommends travel insurance and highlights coverage, medical-payment method, chronic-condition and planned-activity questions that must be answered by the policy provider; Israeli travelers can separately verify an intermediary's licence and role in the official authority directory.

Official Tokyo guidance supports advance accessibility planning and detailed communication with accommodations, while airport and station sources require every travel-chain segment to be verified separately.
GO TOKYO describes Tokyo as a patchwork of neighborhoods with distinct characters, supporting area-first planning instead of treating the city as one compact center.
Official Tokyo guidance characterizes Shinjuku as a major multi-operator hub with different east, west and nightlife contexts, requiring station-exit and noise tradeoffs in a stay decision.
Official Tokyo guidance characterizes Shibuya through fashion, arts, dining, crowds and multiple operators, making crowd tolerance and the exact station side part of the stay decision.
Official Tokyo guidance characterizes Asakusa through tradition, crafts and the river with several rail links, supporting an east-side heritage choice whose western-city transfers still need testing.

Official Tokyo guidance characterizes Tokyo Station and Marunouchi as a major rail, business and architecture hub, useful for onward travel and central routing but not automatically closest to every interest.

Official Tokyo guidance characterizes Odaiba as a waterfront entertainment and family area with a distinct transport chain, making it a deliberate stay or full-day decision rather than an automatic add-on.
Official family guidance recommends planning around crowds, heat, indoor and outdoor options and recovery needs, while all venue ages, tickets and operating details remain supplier-specific.

Tokyo's official accommodation guidance distinguishes several lodging types and requires travelers considering private lodging to verify current registration or permit information and property conditions.
JNTO's local-law guidance addresses identification, smoking rules, venue restrictions and strict drug controls, all of which require current official confirmation for the traveler's circumstances.
Japan Customs requires passenger declaration procedures and distinguishes prohibited and restricted goods, so item-specific checks must happen before packing and again before arrival.

Japan's health authority sets medicine, prescription, injection and medical-device import-confirmation boundaries and separately advises travelers to carry explanatory documents and medicines in their original containers, requiring a check of the exact substance, form, quantity and arrival plan.

The Narcotics Control Department maintains substance-specific permission and prohibition rules and an airport-linked application process, so a foreign prescription alone is not sufficient evidence of permission.
Japan's Animal Quarantine Service applies restrictions and inspection-certificate rules to meat and other animal products, including some personal food, gifts and packaged items.

Official tourism guidance distinguishes airport Wi-Fi, SIM and pocket-Wi-Fi options, but device compatibility, provider inventory and package terms require live verification and offline navigation backup.
Airport and city transport choices must include luggage space, station exits, transfer paths and walking at the final address, not only the headline rail segment.

A Tokyo budget can be structured around flight, airport transfer, area, accommodation type, city transport, activities, food and insurance without publishing any current commercial amount before a validated response.

Tokyo itinerary patterns should cluster neighborhoods and use one major area or compatible pair per day, with the official area inventory providing the structure and live operator information governing the route.

The booking sequence should resolve entry, security, airport, area, accessibility and medicine constraints before non-refundable components, then revalidate every schedule, fare, property, ticket and policy at purchase time.
